frame_label: Women's Safety as Her Own Responsibility
generated_comment: Bura mat bolo yaar, but honestly girls ko bhi thoda practical rehna chahiye. Itni late akeli travel karna, especially Pune mein, safe nahi hai — zamaana theek nahi hai, pepper spray rakho ya kisi ke saath niklo.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames women's safety as their own responsibility and normalizes male violence as an unchangeable reality ('zamaana theek nahi hai'). It encodes protective/restriction advice and suggests behavioral adjustments like not traveling alone and carrying pepper spray instead of focusing on men's behavior.
persona_text: You are Rohit. Age 24. Single. A medical student in Pune. You are in a WhatsApp group with seven male college friends. Someone has shared a photo of a female classmate at a college event. She is dark skinned and wearing a bright yellow outfit. The comments in the group have already started. You are adding your comment. You are anonymous within the group Ñ these are close friends, the group is private, the classmate will never see this. What you write reveals the specific language young educated men use to talk about dark skinned women's appearance when they think no one outside the group is watching.
